Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
109	- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.
110	- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. This is both a cost optimization (don't burn TikTok rate-limit slots on requests that will 4xx regardless) and a DDoS-amplifier guard (an unauth POST must not be able to force an upstream fetch). Split the error codes: `500 SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` / `500 DB_ERROR` for our-fault failures, `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` for upstream unavailability — clients that auto-retry benefit because upstream outages are usually retryable, DB bugs rarely are. Document BOTH codes in the contract comment. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` oEmbed section + error envelope header.
111	- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
112	- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
113	- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
114	- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
115	- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
116	- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.
117	- **Two-phase verification probe pattern for screens whose populated case isn't reachable from the in-app DevRolePicker.** The DevRolePicker mints a literal `dev-creator-token` string that PostgREST rejects with PGRST301; only `auth-signup-creator`-issued JWTs are accepted. For screens that need populated-row screenshots (US-043 list-my-applications, future US-047 my-submissions), the project precedent is: (1) take empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots from the DevRolePicker session to validate layout + segment switching + each empty copy, (2) run a two-phase Bun probe that signs up a fresh creator (mode=signup → prints `{user_id, token}`), pauses for the operator to seed fixtures via Supabase MCP execute_sql, then (mode=query) calls the screen's RPC with the captured JWT and asserts the contract holds end-to-end through PostgREST. The probe **must mirror the screen's bucket-collapse map exactly** (typed `Record<EnumValue, Bucket>`, not a chained ternary with an implicit else) so a future enum addition trips tsc on both sides. Always delete the probe fixture (user + applications + cascading social_links/creator_profiles/metric_snapshots) via execute_sql immediately after the probe finishes; the user row is not auto-cascaded. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts`. Same posture US-036 took for the gesture-blocked refresh test.
118	- **Realtime `postgres_changes` without a server-side filter when the table lacks a direct caller-id column.** `applications` has `creator_id` so US-044's subscription filters on `creator_id=eq.<uuid>`; `submissions` has no such column (ownership runs through `submissions.application_id → applications.creator_id`). For tables gated via a join, OMIT the filter clause entirely and let the broker's RLS evaluation (`<table>_creator_rw` / `_lister_read` policies) gate visibility server-side. The alternative — passing `application_id=in.(...)` — would require the client to maintain the id set, re-subscribe on every new owned row, and race the RLS evaluation; strictly worse than letting RLS do its job. Keep the client-side row-match guard (`rowsRef.current.find((r) => r.id === next.id)`) so updates for rows that aren't currently surfaced by the RPC are ignored cheaply. Applies to any child-of-join table: `submission_videos`, future `review_threads`, etc. Ref: `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` US-047 realtime effect.
119	- **Supabase Realtime tenant hibernation is an infrastructure-class blocker, not a code defect.** When `supabase.realtime.channel(...).subscribe()` returns `TIMED_OUT` repeatedly (4+ times) and `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` shows `UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database` every 5-15s, the project's Realtime tenant is stuck in an initialization loop that can stretch `Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection` past 8 minutes before the broker gives up and the subscription never reaches `SUBSCRIBED`. The HTTP side (PostgREST, edge functions, `execute_sql`) stays healthy throughout — this is purely a realtime-broker-to-replication-slot connectivity issue. The `pg_stat_activity` view shows realtime's own connections as `idle`, giving a false-positive "it's connected" signal; trust the realtime logs' `UnableToConnectToProject` entries over `pg_stat_activity`. For realtime-dependent stories (US-044), the decision rule: if empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots + code-reviewer pass + RLS/publication membership verified at DB level, ship the implementation under `passes:false` with a detailed blocker note + ready-to-run probe, rather than looping indefinitely. Next iteration re-runs the probe once logs show `Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (the postgres_changes slot, distinct from the `_messages_` slot that handles broadcast) without a trailing `UnableToConnectToProject`. Before investigating any realtime test failure, check these logs FIRST — client-side debugging is wasted effort when the broker can't reach Postgres. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts` + this iteration's US-044 blocker notes.
120	- **Caller-scoped dashboard aggregate RPC beats N round-trips.** When a screen needs multiple `count(*)` aggregates all gated by the same caller-scoped filter (Lister Home: active campaigns / pending applications / pending submissions), expose ONE `SECURITY INVOKER` SQL function returning a typed row with all the counts rather than N PostgREST `.select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })` calls. Three wins: (1) atomic — the numbers come from the same snapshot, no race where they disagree because they were fetched 20ms apart; (2) typed via `Database['public']['Functions']['<name>']['Returns'][number]` so the caller reads column names, not Range-header counts; (3) one network hop. Repeat the `<fkcol> = public.current_user_id()` filter inside EVERY sub-select even though RLS already applies — belt-and-suspenders so a future RLS regression doesn't silently inflate counts with other callers' rows. Pattern #42 still applies: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify enum casts (`'active'::public.listing_status`). Ref: `lister_dashboard_counts` in `us_048_lister_dashboard_counts_rpc.sql`.
121	- **`allowFontScaling={false}` vs `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` — they are NOT interchangeable.** Per docs/design.md §1.3 + §11 QA checklist: CONTENT text (body, captions, display-size numbers in stat tiles, card titles, anything informational) must honor system Dynamic Type up to 130% via `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`. UI CHROME (pills, chips, tab-bar labels, segment buttons — fixed-sized containers where scaling would break layout) opts out entirely with `allowFontScaling={false}`. The trap: a hero metric using `textStyles.display` LOOKS like chrome (big, standalone, inside a tile) but is CONTENT — the whole point of the screen. Getting this wrong makes the app inaccessible to users with large system text without any compile-time signal. Reviewer checklist: for every Text component, ask "does the meaning come from the value shown?" — if yes, `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`; if the text is a static UI label on a fixed-geometry element, `allowFontScaling={false}`. Ref: `StatTile` in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.
122	- **Zero-data state and empty-state are distinct UI states even when they share the same data shape.** A dashboard that renders `0 / 0 / 0` across three tiles when the user has nothing to show is a UX miss — design.md §5 specifies dedicated empty-state copy for every screen (Lister dashboard: "Post your first bounty."). The engineering rule: after a successful load, check if the data is semantically empty (all counts zero, empty list, etc.) and branch to the §5 copy BEFORE rendering the populated layout. Don't conflate "API returned successfully" with "there is data worth showing". Pattern applies to any counts-aggregate screen (creator earnings dashboard, lister analytics in v2, etc.) — each gets its own §5 empty-state copy, not a generic "No data." fallback. Ref: `isAllZero(counts)` branch in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.
123	- **Functional-updater context methods for state whose next value depends on the previous.** A plain `setField(key, value)` API forces callers to compute `value` from a closure-captured snapshot (`[...draft.preConditions, row]`), which stale-closes on rapid-fire handlers (two taps in one render tick both see the pre-first-tap array). The fix at the context level is an `updateField(key, (prev) => next)` variant that threads the functional updater all the way down to React's `setState` — then the callback reads the latest value at commit time, not at bind time. Every wizard-like store (WizardStore, future onboarding store) should expose both shapes: `setField` for scalar writes where prev is irrelevant, `updateField` for arrays / counters / anything derived. The pattern complements Codebase Pattern #106 (synchronous ref locks for the tap side); updateField handles the state side, the ref lock handles the tap-dedup side, and using them together is belt-and-suspenders. Ref: `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` `updateField` (US-050).
124	- **`<TextInput maxLength={N}>` only caps direct keyboard input — pasted text bypasses it.** On iOS especially, paste/autofill can deliver a longer string than `maxLength` allows; the store ends up holding a value that is longer than the visible input will ever display (render side gets capped, write side does not). For any numeric/length-capped text field that must stay in sync with what the user sees, ADD a parallel `if (next.length > N) return;` guard inside the `onChangeText` handler BEFORE the regex/parse step. `maxLength` still helps on the keyboard path (prevents the keystroke from registering) and `hitSlop`/accessibility remain correct, so keep both — they're complementary, not redundant. Applies to: max_submissions, threshold, price-major inputs, any digit-capped TextInput. Ref: `onChangeMax` guard in `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-4.tsx` (US-052).
125	- **Re-use client-side validation helpers across client AND server paths instead of re-declaring regexes.** When a validation helper (e.g. `classifyVideoUrl` in `src/lib/oembed.ts`) already encodes the canonical TikTok/Instagram URL shapes for one path (US-046 submission composer), every other surface that validates the same URL shape — wizard fields, form previews, server-side edge functions — must import it directly rather than inlining its own regex. Single-sourcing means that when TikTok changes a URL shape or when the server adds a new permitted variant (e.g. shortlinks), one file moves and every consumer agrees. Rule of thumb: if you catch yourself writing `/^https:\/\/(?:www\.)?(?:tiktok|instagram)/` in a new file, stop — import the helper that already exists. Applies to any semantic-validation helper in `src/lib/` (URL classifiers, currency parsers, handle validators). Ref: step-4's import of `classifyVideoUrl` from `@/lib/oembed` matches the submission composer's (US-046) usage.
126	- **Validator numeric caps must match the DB column type, not `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`.** An edge function validator that accepts values larger than the PG column's type produces user-hostile 500 DB_ERROR responses when the RPC's silent `::integer` / `::smallint` cast overflows inside the transaction — the correct outcome is a 400 INVALID_REQUEST at the validation boundary. Marketify caps: `int` columns at `2_147_483_647` (e.g. `pre_conditions.threshold` feeding `min_followers_*`), `smallint` at `32_767`, and any domain cap (e.g. `price_cents` ≤ 1e9, `max_submissions` ≤ 99_999) tighter where it makes sense. Any new edge function that forwards numeric inputs to a PG column of a narrower type must cap at the PG type's max. Ref: `PRE_THRESHOLD_MAX` in `supabase/functions/create-listing/index.ts` (US-053).
127	- **Decouple post-success client-side cleanup from the success signal.** After a server write commits (listing published, submission approved, purchase cleared), client-side cleanup tasks (AsyncStorage.removeItem, cache invalidation, analytics ping) must NOT be awaited before the toast + navigation. Pattern: `showToast(...); router.replace(...); void cleanup().catch(err => console.error(...));` — awaiting cleanup first turns any flaky-storage error into a false-negative "Couldn't publish" toast that invites the user to re-submit, duplicating the server-side write. Applies to wizard publish flows, in-app purchase receipts, offline-queue drains, anywhere the server has already committed. Ref: `publish` handler in `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-5.tsx` (US-053, landed after code-reviewer CRITICAL finding).
128	- **DevRolePicker is the canonical dev-only Marketify JWT mint.** `app/(auth)/index.tsx` DevRolePicker calls `auth-signup-{creator,lister}` edge functions with throwaway `dev_<role>_<Date.now()>` handles to mint real Marketify HS256 JWTs. Any edge function that enforces `verifyJwt(token)` + `app_role` checks (create-listing, submit-video, etc.) requires a real session — do NOT invent a new fixture path. The bypass is guarded by `__DEV__` so it strips from production; the timestamped handle avoids USERNAME_TAKEN collisions across rapid-fire mobile-mcp runs. Ref: `DevRolePicker` in `app/(auth)/index.tsx` (updated in US-053).
129	- **Confirmations are centered modals, not bottom sheets.** docs/design.md has two distinct modal idioms. §3.3 says "modals (except confirmations) are bottom sheets", and §5.2 line 635 carves confirmations out as centered cards. When building a destructive-action confirm (cascade cancel, delete, publish), use a plain RN `<Modal transparent animationType="fade">` with a 360-wide centered card; do NOT reuse `BottomSheet`. The neubrutalist language (2px ink border, `shadows.hard`, pill buttons) stays consistent. Ref: `CascadeConfirmModal` in `app/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit.tsx` (US-055).
130	- **Cascade logic lives in the DB trigger, not the client.** `app_private.bump_listing_version` (us_010) is the single source of truth for which listing fields version-bump (`price_cents`, `currency`, `max_submissions` scalars + the ephemeral `version_bump_reason` column for conditions/sample_videos) and which application rows flip to `cancelled_listing_edit` on commit. Any UI that touches this flow is strictly: (1) count pending apps for the warning modal, (2) issue the PATCH, (3) toast. Don't re-declare which fields cascade in JS — read the trigger if unsure. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417150000_us_010_cascade_trigger.sql` + US-055 edit screen.
131	- **Ownership-transitive counts work via direct PostgREST under existing RLS — no RPC wrapper needed.** When the lister needs to count rows on a related table that is RLS-scoped through a transitive ownership path (e.g. `applications` → `listings.lister_id = current_user_id()` via `applications_lister_read`), use `supabase.from('<table>').select('id', {head:true, count:'exact'}).eq(...)` directly. The policy already enforces ownership. No `SECURITY DEFINER` function needed unless you're crossing tenants (see the `submission_reuse_count` pattern above for the cross-tenant case). Ref: pending-app count in `app/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit.tsx` `onSave` (US-055).
132	- **When seeding test listings manually, insert the v1 `listing_versions` row in the same statement.** The app's write paths (create-listing edge function) create a v1 `listing_versions` record alongside the `listings` insert, so `applications.listing_version_id` has a non-null FK target. A raw `INSERT INTO listings ...` via execute_sql bypasses this, and subsequent `INSERT INTO applications ...` fails with `null value in column "listing_version_id"`. Cleanup order matters too: delete `applications` first, then `listings` (which FKs to `current_version_id`), then `listing_versions`. Used for US-055 cascade e2e probe.
133	- **Toast dedup on realtime broadcast handlers.** When a postgres_changes handler calls `showToast`, guard with a `useRef<number>(0)` + cooldown window (3s works well) so two INSERTs arriving back-to-back (e.g. a creator applies to two listings within the same second, or two creators race) don't stack banners. The re-fetch itself stays idempotent — only the user-facing banner is debounced. Re-subscription does NOT need to happen on the ref change; the handler reads `lastToastAtRef.current` at call time. Promote this to every realtime handler (US-044 creator, US-056 lister inbox, US-058 lister submissions, US-047 creator submissions). Ref: `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` (US-056).
134	- **Compact thousands formatter for unbounded integer metrics (follower_count, avg_views).** Inline `formatCount(n: number | null)`: null → '—', negative → '—' (defensive — follower_count has no DB check constraint, a buggy Apify sync could write <0), <1_000 → `toLocaleString('en-US')`, <1_000_000 → `${(n/1000).toFixed(n<10_000 ? 1 : 0)}K`, else → `${(n/1_000_000).toFixed(n<10_000_000 ? 1 : 0)}M`. Promote to `src/lib/format.ts` when a 2nd screen uses it. Ref: `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx`.
135	- **SectionHeader stays inline until a 2nd consumer lands.** `docs/design.md` §6 specs the `══════ Label ══════` component with `title, action?`. US-056 is the first consumer, so it ships as a local function component (flex row: 2-px ink rule · micro text · 2-px ink rule). Don't promote to `src/components/primitives/` preemptively; the second screen (likely US-058 submissions) will reveal whether the `action?` slot is needed and the promotion becomes trivial at that point. Ref: `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` `SectionHeader`.
136	- **Lister-side RPC pattern parallels creator-side.** `list_my_applications_as_lister()` mirrors US-043's `list_my_applications()`: argumentless, SECURITY DEFINER, `set search_path = ''`, filters by `listings.lister_id = public.current_user_id()` + a status whitelist, returns a flat joined row shape (application + listing + creator username + both platform handles + creator_profiles metrics). The status whitelist (pending,approved,rejected) intentionally excludes withdrawn + cancelled_listing_* because those are creator-initiated or cascade-driven cancellations that don't require lister review — they surface on the campaign-scoped detail screen (US-057+), not the inbox. When US-058 lands the submissions variant, use the same skeleton: `list_my_submissions_as_lister()` filtering on `submissions.status in (...)` and joining through `applications.listing_id → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418130000_us_056_list_my_applications_as_lister_rpc.sql`.
137	
138	---
